info.title: numbrs API
info.description: REST API for numbrs — personal infrastructure monitoring. All endpoints require an `X-API-KEY` header. Get your API key at https://numbrs.lol/api-keys.

relay_url.description: Associate with a relay by URL (auto-created if new)

tags.name: Integrations
tags.description: Enable and manage server-side data integrations

tags.name: Integrations
tags.description: Enable and manage server-side data integrations

get.description: Returns all relays for the authenticated user.

post.description: Creates a dashboard pre-populated with panels for a specific use case.

template.description: relay-health / relay-overview: Nostr relay monitoring | network-health: network-wide stats | zap-economy: Lightning zaps | protocol-stats: Nostr event analytics | nostr-relays: self-hosted relay DB/pubkey stats | bitaxe: Bitcoin mining | system-health / mac-mini-health: CPU/RAM/disk | uptime-overview: uptime % and latency | media: Plex+Jellyfin combined | plex-media-server: Plex only | github-project: single repo stats | github-projects: multi-repo | vercel-site: deploy tracking | personal-overview: BTC + Nostr + GitHub | personal-stats: custom personal metrics | claude-usage: Claude Code token usage and cost tracking

delete.description: Deletes the dashboard and all its panels.

patch.description: Update any field including `enabled` (maps to `is_active`) to pause/resume alerting.

get.description: Returns the authenticated user's profile and resource counts.

post.description: Ingests one or more datapoints. Metrics and relays are auto-created if they don't exist.
Enable a server-side integration. Upserts by provider — safe to call multiple times.

post.summary: Add a panel to a dashboard

get.description: Returns all metric keys in your account. Useful for discovering available data when building custom dashboards.
Returns Claude Code session data. Data appears on the /claude page.

post.description: Push one or more Claude Code sessions. Upserts by session_id — safe to re-push. Data appears on the /claude page.
